completed_tasks.name: Create backend venv, install packages, pre-download Whisper weights
Attempt FlashAttention 2 install and record the outcome
Write HTTPS probe server and HTTPS setup doc skeleton
Build whisper_bench.py, reference transcript, and unit tests
Run the real Whisper benchmark on the builder recording
Build tts_ttfa.py, short reference transcript, and unit tests
Run the real TTS TTFA benchmark on the builder recording
Build llm_cancel.py and parser/idleness unit tests
Extend TTS probes so backend selection and labeling are explicit
Re-run labeled TTS measurements on OMEN-PC and persist the backend matrix
Update handoff/writeback inputs to cite backend-labeled numbers

remaining_tasks.name: Verify trusted HTTPS from the actual Android device
Build vram_soak.py + unit tests for the growth detector
Run 30-minute VRAM soak for F5, XTTS, and Qwen3 on OMEN-PC
Run the cancel probe against the existing LLM endpoint at 192.168.1.190:8001 from a shell that can observe its GPU
Write final Phase 0 synthesis and write back PROJECT.md / STATE.md

blockers: Android browser verification is still pending for 00-02 Task 2.
00-06 no longer needs a new server install, but the cancel probe still needs to run from a shell that can observe the GPU used by the existing endpoint at 192.168.1.190:8001.

WSL access on 192.168.1.199 currently requires the pmpg Windows account; the rayme-ssh account reports no installed WSL distributions and remote WSL work should use direct `wsl -d Ubuntu -e sh -c ...` commands with fixed Linux paths.
Phase 00 writeback (00-08) is blocked until 00-02, 00-05, and 00-06 provide their missing inputs.
No measured TTS engine clears the v1 latency budget on OMEN-PC: F5 is 517.3 ms via simulated chunk streaming, XTTS native streaming is 534.3 ms, and Qwen3 eager baseline is 5626.8 ms.
FlashAttention 2 still fails to build on OMEN-PC, so Qwen3 remains an eager-attention baseline only.
human_actions_pending: Run the HTTPS probe from the actual Android device and confirm trusted cert + isSecureContext for 00-02 Task 2.
Keep the existing OpenAI-compatible server at 192.168.1.190:8001 running and provide the execution path that can observe its GPU with nvidia-smi during 00-06 Task 2.

decisions.decision: Keep OMEN-PC as the only valid Phase 0 execution host and restore SSH from the repo-persisted key each session.
decisions.rationale: The earlier SEV-1 came from running backend work on the wrong machine and relying on disposable SSH state.

decisions.decision: Treat F5 and XTTS as not_applicable for attention backend labeling in the current probe.
decisions.rationale: Neither probe path exposes eager, SDPA, or FlashAttention selection; their runtime differences are streaming-path differences, not attention-backend switches.

decisions.decision: Treat Qwen3-TTS as an eager-attention baseline until flash_attn imports successfully on OMEN-PC.
decisions.rationale: The fresh labeled rerun and the FA2 install verdict both confirm that flash-attn is unavailable in this runtime.

decisions.decision: Use tts_attention_matrix.json as the source of truth for optimization backend labeling in future writeback.
decisions.rationale: The old tts_ttfa.json schema could be misread as an optimized Qwen result; the dedicated matrix makes the backend status explicit per engine.

decisions.decision: Use `http://192.168.1.190:8001/v1` with model `unsloth/Qwen3.5-27B` as the current Phase 0 LLM endpoint for plan 00-06.
decisions.rationale: The endpoint is now reachable from the current workspace, returns healthy status, and exposes an OpenAI-compatible models list.

decisions.decision: Use the pmpg Windows account for WSL-backed work on OMEN-PC; rayme-ssh remains valid for native Windows backend work.
decisions.rationale: Verified on 2026-04-22: pmpg sees Ubuntu on WSL2 and can run `wsl ls /`, while rayme-ssh reports no installed WSL distributions.

decisions.decision: For remote WSL work on OMEN-PC, prefer direct `wsl -d Ubuntu -e sh -c ...` commands with fixed Linux paths and avoid UNC WSL paths from the SSH session.
decisions.rationale: Verified on 2026-04-22: `/home/pmpg/rayme-wsl-probe` was created successfully inside Ubuntu, while `\\wsl.localhost\Ubuntu\...` and `\\wsl$\Ubuntu\...` were not reliably available from the non-interactive SSH session.

decisions.decision: Use `/home/pmpg/rayme/.venv-cu121` with `CUDA_HOME=/usr/local/cuda-12.1` as the reusable WSL accelerator env on OMEN-PC.
decisions.rationale: Verified on 2026-04-22: the env imports `torch 2.5.1+cu121` with CUDA visible on the RTX 3060 and also imports `deepspeed 0.18.9` successfully.

decisions.decision: Treat the upgraded OMEN-PC WSL env as both DeepSpeed-capable and FlashAttention-capable for future Linux-side probes.
decisions.rationale: Verified on 2026-04-23 after the in-place distro upgrade to Ubuntu 22.04.5 and glibc 2.35: `/home/pmpg/rayme/.venv-cu121` imports `torch 2.5.1+cu121`, `triton 3.1.0`, `deepspeed 0.18.9`, and `flash-attn 2.8.3` successfully on the RTX 3060.

decisions.decision: Keep F5-TTS as the current v1 default candidate while retaining XTTS as compatibility fallback.
decisions.rationale: No engine meets the target budget, and the latest OMEN-PC rerun still leaves F5 with the best TTFA while XTTS remains the only native streamer.

decisions.decision: Use the OMEN-PC WSL Docker Desktop Triton/TensorRT-LLM path as the current maximum-speed F5 runtime path, with a safe assembled model repo and CPU-preferred BLS response memory for the vocoder hop.
decisions.rationale: Verified on 2026-04-23: the stock prebuilt runtime image works after staging artifacts and applying the generated model repo patch that avoids the failing CUDA IPC output path under WSL.

next_action: If continuing autonomous work on Phase 00, run ./scripts/bootstrap-rayme-ssh.sh restore and execute 00-05-vram-soak on OMEN-PC so Phase 00 has the missing 3060 budget data. For maximum-speed F5 follow-up work on `rayme-pmpg`, the validated WSL Triton path is now scripted under .planning/spikes/002-f5-triton-trtllm-wsl-path/ and the next meaningful benchmark is to compare this runtime against the existing native Windows TTFA baseline. In parallel or before final writeback, the builder still needs to complete Android HTTPS verification for 00-02 and provide the GPU-observable execution path for 00-06 against `http://192.168.1.190:8001/v1`.
context_notes: 00-07.1 is now complete. The TTS probe and matrix explicitly label backend support: F5=not_applicable, XTTS=not_applicable, Qwen3=eager baseline with flash_attention_2 unavailable and sdpa not supported by the current probe. Fresh OMEN-PC rerun metrics are F5 517.3 ms / 0.388 RTF / 1990.2 MB, XTTS 534.3 ms / 0.59 RTF / 1897.6 MB, Qwen3 5626.8 ms / 2.705 RTF / 2509.1 MB. Remote unit tests for test_tts_ttfa.py now pass at 12/12 in the Phase 0 backend venv. 00-08 must cite the matrix instead of describing Qwen3 as an unlabeled result. The Phase 0 LLM endpoint is now known: `http://192.168.1.190:8001/v1`, health OK, model `unsloth/Qwen3.5-27B`, OpenAI-compatible, `owned_by: llamacpp`. On 192.168.1.199, WSL access is tied to the pmpg Windows account: `pmpg` sees `Ubuntu` on WSL2, SSH as `omen-pc\pmpg` now works via `C:\ProgramData\ssh\administrators_authorized_keys`, and `/home/pmpg/rayme-wsl-probe` was created successfully inside Ubuntu. Remote WSL commands should use direct `wsl -d Ubuntu -e sh -c ...` invocations with fixed Linux paths; UNC WSL paths were not reliable from the non-interactive SSH session. The WSL distro upgrade is complete: Ubuntu is now 22.04.5 with glibc 2.35, and the reusable WSL accelerator env `/home/pmpg/rayme/.venv-cu121` imports `torch 2.5.1+cu121`, `triton 3.1.0`, `deepspeed 0.18.9`, and `flash-attn 2.8.3` on the RTX 3060. `.planning/spikes/001-omen-pc-wsl-gpu-path/bootstrap-cu121-env.sh` now recreates that stack directly. The F5 Triton/TensorRT-LLM WSL path also now passes end to end: image `soar97/triton-f5-tts:24.12`, workspace `/home/pmpg/rayme/f5-triton-runtime/F5-TTS`, safe model repo `/home/pmpg/rayme/f5-triton-runtime/model_repo_cpuipc_18000`, host ports `18000/18001/18002`, and output `/home/pmpg/rayme/f5-triton-runtime/client_http_out.wav`. The stock prebuilt image still needs ad hoc `vocos` for stage 2 and `rjieba` for runtime launch, and the generated model repo copy must force the `vocoder` BLS response to CPU preferred memory to avoid the WSL CUDA IPC failure. Hard safety rule: do not use `rm -rf` and do not delete files or directories through variable-expanded paths on any backend or WSL host.

incident.summary: Phase 0 execution previously ran on the wrong machine and lost SSH persistence, forcing repeated recovery loops.
incident.user_impact: Multiple days of avoidable churn and justified loss of trust.
prevention_rules: Use OMEN-PC only for Phase 0 backend work.
Restore SSH from .local/phase0-ssh/ each fresh session.
Verify hostname, whoami, python --version, and nvidia-smi before claiming backend access works.
Prefer the shortest validated recovery path.
Never run rm -rf on project or backend hosts.
Never delete files or directories through variable-expanded paths.
